p.em: Intel Manual Quality Reference for Sandy Bridge through Skylake Microarchitectures

ul.li: CLEAN → DIRTY: Any 256-bit AVX instruction triggered by writing to YMM[255:128].
DIRTY + SSE: 70-cycle stall on Sandy Bridge, Ivy Bridge, Haswell, Broadwell.
Best Practice: Always execute _mm256_zeroupper() before calling external code or library functions that might use SSE.
Sandy Bridge–Broadwell: Severe penalty (~70 cycles) due to internal state mismatches between AVX and SSE execution units.
Skylake: Penalty reduced to ~5 cycles, but code portability dictates vzeroupper usage.
Zen 2 / Zen 3: No hardware penalty, however vzeroupper is recommended for cross-vendor optimization.
